headline: Record 73 Million Children Vaccinated in World's Poorest Nations
meta_description: The world's lowest-income countries vaccinated a record 73 million children in 2025, restoring vaccine coverage to near pre-pandemic levels.
summary_short: Low-income countries achieved a vaccination record in 2025, immunizing 73 million children—the highest number in history, reversing pandemic setbacks.
body:
Global childhood vaccination efforts have reached a historic peak. In 2025, the world's lowest-income countries vaccinated a record 73 million children—surpassing any previous year on record.

This achievement is particularly significant because it represents a recovery from pandemic disruptions that had reduced vaccination rates in poorer regions. By restoring coverage to levels approaching the pre-pandemic era, health programs have prevented a dangerous erosion of immunity to preventable diseases.

The milestone reflects enormous effort by health workers, community organizations, and international partners working together to reach children in remote and underserved areas. Each vaccination represents protection not only for an individual child but for entire communities through herd immunity. The record shows that even in resource-limited settings, sustained commitment to vaccination saves lives and builds healthier futures.
